milestone: EPA compliance newsletter scope research — reviewed national water utility regulatory landscape, identified content pillars for compliance-angle digest targeting municipalities, utilities, and institutional clients
tldr:
Researched EPA regulatory landscape as the content foundation for Raybern's national newsletter. Identified 3 core pillars: compliance updates, case studies, and utility trend data.

• National scope confirmed — no state/regional variants
• Primary content sources: EPA enforcement actions, Safe Drinking Water Act updates, utility audit findings
• Mapped content freshness cycle to quarterly regulatory release windows
• Audience spans municipalities, utility operators, universities, industrial clients

milestone: Newsletter content strategy defined — national scope finalized, audience stakeholder map built, Google Trends public data approach scoped as content engine for national EPA compliance digest
tldr:
Finalized the newsletter's strategic framework — national scope, public data surfacing via Google Trends-style signals, designed for multiple stakeholder types across municipalities, utilities, universities, and industrial clients.

• Audience: all stakeholder types across 50 states — no regional segmentation
• Data model: public EPA/SDWA data + trend signals + Raybern team case study inserts
• Format: compliance digest with wins section showcasing Raybern client outcomes
• Fits into Growth Hub as the third pillar alongside Lead Intelligence and Webinars

milestone: Scoped the process-transparency-as-billing-foundation approach — worked through how to make work visible (thinking → research → strategy → execution → deliverable) without day-by-day hour matching; established Growth Hub dashboard changelog model as the solution
tldr: Scoped the 'show your work' model — every named step in the process (thinking, research, design, build, test) gets its own dated entry, creating a visible body of work without explicit hour claims.

milestone: Newsletter work from last week formalized as distinct work log entries — EPA compliance scope research (Apr 28) and national content strategy session (Apr 29) added to Growth Hub dashboard
tldr:
Identified two undocumented newsletter deliverables from last week and added them as named entries to the Growth Hub work log.
Completes the Apr 27–May 1 work story with newsletter development properly represented alongside the LinkedIn calendar and strategy docs.

• Apr 28 entry: EPA compliance newsletter scope research (Research category)
• Apr 29 entry: National newsletter content strategy — scope, audience map, Google Trends data model (Strategy category)
• Brought Apr 27–May 1 billed hour log to 13 hrs total — within the agreed weekly cap

milestone: TLDR format standard scoped and approved — all work log entries must open with 2 sentences or 2–4 bullets summarizing the work before any process detail
tldr:
Designed and got approval on a TLDR-first format for all work log notes — makes the dashboard scannable at a glance without requiring clients to read full process detail.
Applied to all new entries going forward and backfilled to existing entries covering Apr 13–May 4.

• Format: 2 sentences OR 2–4 bullets (whichever fits the entry type) at the top of notes, followed by a blank line, then any additional detail
• No hours anywhere — process steps and deliverables only
• Daily cron task updated to enforce the standard on all future entries

milestone: TLDR backfill executed — 22 dashboard entries (Apr 13–May 4) updated with TLDR-first notes format via automated script
tldr:
Wrote and ran a backfill script that updated 22 entries across Apr 13–May 4, prepending a TLDR summary to each entry's notes field while preserving all existing detail.
All entries now follow the TLDR-first format — consistent across the full 3-week date range.

• Script: backfill_tldr.py — 22 targeted UPDATE statements, one per entry ID
• Entries already in TLDR format (ids 64, 65 — newsletter entries) were skipped
• Verified 22/22 rows updated successfully before closing
